query:
INSERT INTO public.vocabulary_words (word, definition, example_sentence, part_of_speech, difficulty, exam_type)
VALUES
  ('Universal', 'Present everywhere', '“Human rights should be universal.”', 'adjective', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Univocal', 'Having only one meaning', 'What seemed univocal at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Unkempt', 'Dishevelled, untidy, dirty, not kept up', 'Her unkempt became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Unmitigat', 'Not lessened; absolute', 'The reviewer described the tone as unmitigat, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Unobtrusive', 'Not noticeable or blatant, inconspicuous', 'The passage presents a unobtrusive portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Unoriginal', 'Lacking novelty', '“The movie’s plot felt unoriginal.”', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Unpalatable', 'Not pleasant to taste; difficult to tolerate or accept', 'The author hoped the new policy would unpalatable tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Unprecedent', 'Never seen before', '“The crisis was unprecedented in scale.”', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Unprecedented', 'Never done or known before', 'What seemed unprecedented at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Unpretentious', 'Modest and not trying to impress with a show of wealth or importance', 'Over time, the community learned to unpretentious its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Unravel', 'To separate the threads (of)', 'Critics argued that the plan might unravel the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Unregenerate', 'Which cannot be transformed in mind and spirit', 'The author hoped the new policy would unregenerate tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Unremitt', 'Incessant, never slackening', 'The essay highlights the unremitt of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Unrequit', 'Unanswered, not returned, not reciprocated, not repaid', 'Her unrequit became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Unscath', 'Not harmed or damaged in any way, untouched', 'The passage presents a unscath portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Unscrupulous', 'Conscienceless, unprincipled', 'The reviewer described the tone as unscrupulous, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Unsettle', 'To make upset or uncomfortable', 'Over time, the community learned to unsettle its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Unsophisticat', 'Without complexity or refinements', 'The essay highlights the unsophisticat of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Unspeakable', 'Incapable of being spoken or uttered', 'The reviewer described the tone as unspeakable, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Untenable', 'Not able to be maintained or defended', 'The author hoped the new policy would untenable tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Untimely', 'At an inopportune time', 'He untimely overlooked details that later proved decisive.', 'adverb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Untoward', 'Unfavorable, inappropriate, or inconvenient', 'The essay highlights the untoward of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Unutterable', 'Unspeakable, beyond expression', 'The passage presents a unutterable portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Unwarrant', 'Not justified or authorized', 'The reviewer described the tone as unwarrant, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Unwieldy', 'Not readily handled or managed in use or action, awkward', 'The passage presents a unwieldy portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Unwitt', 'Unintentional', 'The essay highlights the unwitt of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Unwont', 'Not customary or habitual, unusual, infrequent, strange', 'The reviewer described the tone as unwont, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Unyield', 'Not giving way or bending under pressure; rigid or inflexible', 'The reviewer described the tone as unyield, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Unyoke', 'To release something from a yoke or harness', 'Critics argued that the plan might unyoke the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Up-keep', 'The process of properly maintaining something', 'Scholars noted a striking up-keep between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Upbraid', 'Scold or find fault with someone', 'Scholars noted a striking upbraid between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Upcast', 'To cast or throw up, to turn upward', 'The author hoped the new policy would upcast tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Upheaval', 'Strong or violent change or disturbance', 'Her upheaval became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Upheave', 'To heave or lift up, raise up or aloft', 'The author hoped the new policy would upheave tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Uphold', 'To confirm or support something', 'Critics argued that the plan might uphold the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Uppermost', 'At, or nearest the top of something', 'The essay highlights the uppermost of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Upright', 'Vertical, erect', 'Scholars noted a striking upright between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Uproarious', 'In a state of uproar, confused and noisy', 'What seemed uproarious at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Uproot', 'To tear away from a native place or environment', 'Over time, the community learned to uproot its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Upshot', 'The outcome or result of an action', 'Her upshot became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Upstart', 'To rise suddenly, to spring', 'Critics argued that the plan might upstart the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Upturn', 'To turn (something) up or over', 'Over time, the community learned to upturn its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Urban', 'Related to the (or any) city', 'Over time, the community learned to urban its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Urbane', 'Courteous and refined in manner', 'Scholars noted a striking urbane between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Urbanity', 'Behaviour that is polished, refined, courteous', 'The essay highlights the urbanity of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Urchin', 'A mischievous child', 'Her urchin became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Urgency', 'Imperativeness', 'The essay highlights the urgency of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Usage', 'A customary way of doing something', 'The essay highlights the usage of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Usually', 'Most of the time', 'The results were usually linked to changes in public policy.', 'adverb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Usurious', 'Charging illegal rates of interest for the use of money', 'What seemed usurious at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type)
ON CONFLICT (word, exam_type) DO UPDATE SET
  definition = EXCLUDED.definition,
  example_sentence = EXCLUDED.example_sentence,
  part_of_speech = EXCLUDED.part_of_speech,
  difficulty = EXCLUDED.difficulty;
